mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity risk premiums and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production indicate immediate supply chain bottlenecks that force corporate margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative following INDPRO declines exceeding 0.5% in post-shock recovery environments.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL accelerating above 3.5% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns show positive directionality in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment signals labor market deterioration that erodes consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE changes yields a Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day forward returns across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates directly map to corporate revenue trajectories, creating a persistent positive relationship between GDPC1 momentum and short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit no positive correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rates (Rank IC < 0.01 over 5 years).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production outpacing GDP growth indicates supply-side efficiency gains that boost profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds GDPC1 growth during disinflationary expansions.
